title: Tiger Out: Scheffler and McIlroy Duel for the Green Jacket at Masters 2026
excerpt: Tiger Woods has withdrawn from the Masters following his DUI arrest. Scottie Scheffler and defending champion Rory McIlroy are primed for an epic battle at Augusta National.
body:
The Tiger Question is Finally Answered

On Tuesday, Augusta National ended days of speculation: Tiger Woods will not play the 2026 Masters. Chairman Fred Ridley released a statement confirming the five-time champion is stepping away to receive treatment following his March 27 DUI arrest in Florida. A breathalyzer test came back at 0.00 — authorities believe medication was the cause — but the timing and circumstances have overshadowed what should have been a triumphant return. Woods was still just 50 years old, and Augusta had held the Patch, a nine-hole short course he had designed, unveiled just last month. The narrative felt different. Now it simply feels heavier.

Tournament Dates
Apr 9-12
Scheffler Odds
5/1 Favourite
Field Size
93 Players
Masters Titles
Scheffler 2

Scheffler: The Favorite’s Burden

Scottie Scheffler arrives at Augusta as the overwhelming favourite, and for good reason. The world No. 1 withdrew from the Valero Texas Open — this week's final tune-up event — to await the birth of his second child, arriving rested and fully dialled in. His ball-striking is nearly matchless. He has won here twice before (2023, 2024). The greens, elevated and sloped toward his natural iron trajectories, might as well have been built for him.

Yet here’s the rub: defending your own Masters title is one thing. Winning a third in five years is another beast entirely. Augusta has a way of humbling even the best players at the worst moments.

McIlroy: Champion and Free

Rory McIlroy’s 2025 Masters victory was the culmination of a career’s worth of near-misses. The Green Jacket completed his career Grand Slam, lifting a 12-year curse that had bent him mentally and physically. He arrives in 2026 as defending champion — loose, liberated, and playing with a freedom he hasn’t felt at Augusta since his twenties.

If he wins back-to-back, he becomes the first player to do so since Tiger Woods in 2002-03. The omens are good for Rory. The script writes itself.

The Chasing Pack

Cameron Young proved his credentials at The Players Championship with a closing 68 and a 375-yard drive on the 72nd hole. Matt Fitzpatrick, second at Sawgrass, won the Valspar Championship immediately after at -11. Both men possess the precision and ice-cold temperament that Augusta demands. Jon Rahm, having won back-to-back LIV events, is rounding into ominous form just as the Masters arrives.

Thunder Before the Storm

The 2026 Masters has already been stripped of its greatest narrative — the Tiger redemption. What remains is a tournament wide open for the first time in years. Scheffler is the favourite, McIlroy the defending champion. But Augusta National has a way of surprising everyone. The next four days will tell us who truly belongs in green.
